ruleset_version: Regelverk 2 - Operativt verifieringsregelverk för Certificate of Origin

certificate.label: Document A
certificate.quality_notes: Clear text-based PDF. All fields legible. Original certificate (1/1) issued by West Sweden Chamber of Commerce, Göteborg.

invoice.label: Document B
invoice.quality_notes: Clear text-based PDF, 2 pages. All fields legible. Issued by Lantmännen Biorefineries AB.

lc_check.motivation: No Letter of Credit reference found in the certificate. The certificate number is SEG-26D-461827 which does not contain any LC banking term. Verification proceeds with all checkpoint controls.

locations_found: Invoice page 1: top-left issuer block 'Lantmännen Biorefineries AB, Hanholmsvägen 69, 602 38 NORRKÖPING'
Invoice page 1: exporter declaration 'Name of the exporter: Lantmännen Biorefineries AB, Jungmansgatan 8, 53140 Lidköping, Sweden'
Invoice page 1: delivery terms 'FCA Biorefineries AB Lidköping'
Invoice page 1: footer 'Lantmännen Biorefineries AB, Hanholmsvägen 69, 602 38 NORRKÖPING'
Invoice page 2: top-left issuer block 'Lantmännen Biorefineries AB'

criteria_met: Criterion 2: Consignor 'Lantmännen Biorefineries AB' identified as invoice issuer in header, exporter declaration, and footer.
Criterion 3: Consignee 'CHORI CO., LTD' identified in invoice Delivery Address, Customer address, and Customer fields.
pairing_validation.motivation: Both consignor and consignee from the certificate can be identified in the invoice. Document pairing is valid.

checkpoints.certificate_value: Lantmännen Biorefineries AB, Jungmansgatan 8, SE-531 40, LIDKÖPING, Sweden
checkpoints.invoice_value: Lantmännen Biorefineries AB, Hanholmsvägen 69, 602 38 NORRKÖPING (issuer); Also: Lantmännen Biorefineries AB, Jungmansgatan 8, 53140 Lidköping, Sweden (exporter declaration)

comparison_steps.description: Search for consignor name 'Lantmännen Biorefineries AB' anywhere in the invoice.
comparison_steps.result: FOUND. The name 'Lantmännen Biorefineries AB' appears in multiple locations: invoice header/branding, issuer block, exporter declaration, delivery terms, and footer. The name is an EXACT match to the certificate's consignor name after normalization.

comparison_steps.description: Verify that the company name in the certificate matches the invoice issuer/exporter.
comparison_steps.result: MATCH. 'Lantmännen Biorefineries AB' in certificate = 'Lantmännen Biorefineries AB' in invoice (issuer and exporter declaration). Exact name match.

comparison_steps.description: Verify that the country for the consignor matches.
comparison_steps.result: MATCH. Certificate: 'Sweden'. Invoice: VAT prefix 'SE' (= Sweden per rule 4.1.2.1), and exporter declaration explicitly states 'Sweden'. Country verified via both indirect identifier (VAT) and explicit address.

rules_applied.description: Group structure – consignor name found explicitly in invoice
rules_applied.outcome: MATCH – name found in multiple locations

rules_applied.description: Country verification via indirect identifier (VAT prefix SE)

evidence.relevance: Exact match with certificate consignor name

evidence.raw_text: Name of the exporter: Lantmännen Biorefineries AB, Jungmansgatan 8, 53140 Lidköping, Sweden
evidence.relevance: Exact match with certificate consignor name AND address (Jungmansgatan 8, Lidköping)
SE prefix confirms Sweden as country for issuer/consignor
Explicit country in exporter address block

checkpoints.debug_explanation: Certificate consignor: 'Lantmännen Biorefineries AB, Jungmansgatan 8, SE-531 40, LIDKÖPING, Sweden'. Invoice issuer: 'Lantmännen Biorefineries AB, Hanholmsvägen 69, 602 38 NORRKÖPING'. The company name is an exact match. The street address differs (Jungmansgatan 8 in certificate vs Hanholmsvägen 69 in invoice header), but per rule 4.1.5, address differences do not cause MISMATCH — only name and country matter. Furthermore, the exporter declaration on the invoice explicitly states 'Lantmännen Biorefineries AB, Jungmansgatan 8, 53140 Lidköping, Sweden' which matches the certificate's address exactly. Country is Sweden in both, confirmed via VAT prefix SE and explicit text. Rule 4.1.0 is satisfied: consignor name found explicitly in invoice. MATCH.
checkpoints.motivation: Consignor 'Lantmännen Biorefineries AB' is explicitly identified in the invoice as the issuer (header, footer) and as the named exporter in the exporter declaration. The exporter declaration also contains the identical address (Jungmansgatan 8, 53140 Lidköping, Sweden). Country Sweden is confirmed via VAT prefix SE and explicit address. MATCH per rules 4.1.0 and 4.1.2.1.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: MISMATCH due to different street addresses (Jungmansgatan 8 vs Hanholmsvägen 69)
rejected_hypotheses.rejection_reason: Per rule 4.1.5, differences in street address or postal code do not constitute MISMATCH for consignor. The company name and country are the determining factors. Additionally, the exporter declaration in the invoice contains the exact same address as the certificate.

comparison_steps.result: Invoice has 'Customer address' block with 'CHORI CO., LTD' and 'Customer' field with 'CHORI CO., LTD'. These serve as the primary factoring fields. Also 'Delivery Address' block has 'CHORI CO., LTD'.

comparison_steps.description: Verify consignee name from certificate against invoice.
comparison_steps.result: MATCH. Certificate: 'CHORI CO., LTD'. Invoice Customer address: 'CHORI CO., LTD'. Exact match after normalization.
MATCH. Certificate: 'Japan'. Invoice Customer address: 'Japan'. Exact match.

evidence.relevance: Delivery address with same company name, department, and street — confirms same party

checkpoints.debug_explanation: Certificate consignee: 'CHORI CO., LTD, Life Science Dept. (SEC. 0061), 1-7-3 Awajimachi, Chuo-ku Osaka 540-8603, Japan'. Invoice Customer address: 'CHORI CO., LTD, 1-7-3 Awajimachi, Chuo-ku Osaka 540-8603, Japan'. Company name is an exact match. Country Japan matches exactly. The Delivery Address also shows the same company with the department '(SEC. 0061)' matching the certificate. Per rule 4.2.4, address differences do not cause MISMATCH when name and country match. Both primary (Customer address) and secondary (Delivery Address) fields confirm the same party. MATCH.
checkpoints.motivation: Consignee 'CHORI CO., LTD' is explicitly identified in the invoice Customer address block and Delivery Address block. The company name is an exact match. Country Japan is confirmed in both blocks. Department 'Life Science Dept. (SEC. 0061)' also matches in the Delivery Address. MATCH.

comparison_steps.description: Check if certificate contains article numbers.
comparison_steps.result: Certificate does NOT contain an explicit article number. The invoice has item number '100022' but the certificate does not reference this. Priority 1 is not applicable — proceed to Priority 2.

comparison_steps.description: Identify the identity-bearing main designation in the certificate and match against invoice.
comparison_steps.result: Certificate main designation: 'Vital Wheat Gluten 1000 Kg'. Invoice item description: 'Vital Wheat Gluten 1000 Kg'. EXACT match after normalization of case.

rules_applied.description: Controlled text matching – identity-bearing main designation
rules_applied.outcome: MATCH – exact text match of 'Vital Wheat Gluten 1000 Kg'

rules_applied.description: Non-identity-bearing additions – '20 Pallets' and production information in certificate are additional context, not affecting product identity
rules_applied.outcome: Additional text does not cause MISMATCH

evidence.raw_text:
20 Pallets
Vital Wheat Gluten 1000 Kg
From Lantmännen Biorefineries AB
Produced in Sweden with wheat from EU Region

evidence.relevance: Invoice goods description – exact match with certificate main designation

checkpoints.debug_explanation: Certificate goods description (Box 6): '20 Pallets / Vital Wheat Gluten 1000 Kg / From Lantmännen Biorefineries AB / Produced in Sweden with wheat from EU Region'. The identity-bearing main designation is 'Vital Wheat Gluten 1000 Kg'. Invoice item description: 'Vital Wheat Gluten 1000 Kg'. This is an EXACT text match. The additional lines in the certificate (20 Pallets, producer info, production origin) are non-identity-bearing additions per rule 4.3.4 — they do not affect product identity. No article number is given in the certificate, so Priority 1 does not apply. Priority 2 textual matching gives exact match. MATCH.
checkpoints.motivation: The identity-bearing main designation 'Vital Wheat Gluten 1000 Kg' in the certificate exactly matches the invoice item description. Additional certificate text about pallets and production origin does not affect product identity. MATCH via controlled text matching (Priority 2).

comparison_steps.description: Certificate Box 7 contains 'Net 20000 Kg'. This is the verification-basis quantity.
Search for numeric value 20000 with unit KG in the invoice.
comparison_steps.result: Invoice item line: '20,000 KG'. After thousands separator normalization (rule 4.4.3.3), this is 20000 KG. MATCH.

comparison_steps.description: Certificate specifies 'Net' (= NW). Invoice states '20,000 KG' without explicit GW/NW category. Check rule 4.4.2.2.
comparison_steps.result: Certificate states one weight value: Net 20000 Kg. Invoice states one quantity: 20,000 KG without GW/NW label. Invoice does not contain a separate conflicting weight of another category. Per rule 4.4.2.2 point 2: single weight value without category, matched in invoice, no conflicting alternative → MATCH.

rules_applied.outcome: Net 20000 Kg identified as verification basis

rules_applied.description: Thousands separator normalization – comma removed from '20,000'
Weight category – single value, no conflicting alternative in invoice
rules_applied.outcome: MATCH per point 2 (single value, invoice confirms same numeric value with unit, no conflicting alternative)

rules_applied.description: Occurrence check – numeric value 20000 KG found in invoice

evidence.relevance: Invoice quantity – matches certificate after normalization

checkpoints.debug_explanation: Certificate Box 7: 'Net 20000 Kg'. Invoice quantity column: '20,000 KG'. After thousands separator normalization (removing comma per rule 4.4.3.3, both values are integers, comma is unambiguously a thousands separator), the invoice value is 20000 KG. Certificate value: 20000 Kg. Numeric values match exactly. Unit KG matches (case normalization: Kg = KG). Certificate specifies 'Net' (NW). Invoice does not explicitly state GW or NW, but states only one weight value (20,000 KG). Per rule 4.4.2.2 point 2: one weight value in certificate, matched in invoice, invoice does not contain a conflicting alternative weight → MATCH. No summation or conversion needed. MATCH.
checkpoints.motivation: Certificate quantity 'Net 20000 Kg' matches invoice quantity '20,000 KG' after thousands separator normalization. The numeric value (20000) and unit (KG) are identical. The invoice does not contain a conflicting alternative weight value. MATCH.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: MANUAL_REVIEW because certificate says 'Net' but invoice doesn't specify NW
rejected_hypotheses.rejection_reason: Per rule 4.4.2.2 point 2: when certificate states one weight value and this value can be identified in the invoice, and the invoice does NOT contain a conflicting alternative weight value of a different category → MATCH. The invoice only has one quantity (20,000 KG) with no separate GW value, so no ambiguity exists.

checkpoints.invoice_value: SE (Country of Origin column); EU preferential origin (exporter declaration)

comparison_steps.description: Search for explicit origin fields in the invoice.
comparison_steps.result: Found: (1) 'Country of Origin' column on item line: 'SE'. (2) Exporter declaration: 'this products are of EU preferential origin'.

comparison_steps.description: Certificate: 'EU Sweden'. Normalize: 'EU' = European Union. 'Sweden' = SE (ISO alpha-2). Invoice: 'SE' = Sweden. EU preferential origin = European Union.
comparison_steps.result: Both components can be normalized and compared.

comparison_steps.description: Certificate states 'EU Sweden' (EU + one specific EU member state). Invoice has explicit EU origin declaration ('EU preferential origin') in a dedicated exporter field. Per rule 4.5.4.2.1: when certificate states EU combined with ONE specific EU member state, and invoice contains an explicit EU origin statement in a dedicated field, this is MATCH.
comparison_steps.result: MATCH. Certificate 'EU Sweden' is verified against invoice's 'EU preferential origin' declaration and 'SE' Country of Origin.

comparison_steps.description: Additionally verify Sweden specifically: Invoice Country of Origin column shows 'SE'. SE = Sweden (ISO alpha-2). Certificate says 'Sweden'. → Verified.
comparison_steps.result: MATCH. Sweden explicitly confirmed via 'SE' in Country of Origin column.

comparison_steps.step: 4.5.4.1 – Check for non-EU origins in invoice
comparison_steps.description: Verify no non-EU origins in invoice that would conflict with certificate.
comparison_steps.result: Invoice contains only one origin: 'SE' (Sweden, EU member). No non-EU origins found. No conflict.

rules_applied.description: EU [land] exception – certificate states 'EU Sweden', invoice has explicit EU preferential origin declaration
EU member state normalization – Sweden ↔ SE, EU ↔ EU preferential origin
Check for non-EU origins in invoice

evidence.relevance: Explicit origin field – SE = Sweden (EU member state)

evidence.raw_text: this products are of EU preferential origin
evidence.relevance: Explicit EU origin declaration in dedicated exporter reference field

checkpoints.debug_explanation: Certificate Box 3: 'EU Sweden'. This means European Union, specifically Sweden. Invoice has two origin indicators: (1) Country of Origin column value 'SE' (ISO alpha-2 for Sweden), and (2) exporter declaration stating 'EU preferential origin'. Applying rule 4.5.4.2.1: certificate states 'EU [one EU member state]', invoice contains explicit EU origin statement in dedicated exporter field → MATCH. Additionally, rule 4.5.4.2 confirms Sweden ↔ SE normalization and EU ↔ EU preferential origin. No non-EU origins in invoice (rule 4.5.4.1 check passed). Both components of 'EU Sweden' are verified: EU via exporter declaration, Sweden via Country of Origin column 'SE'. MATCH.
checkpoints.motivation: Certificate origin 'EU Sweden' is verified: 'Sweden' confirmed via invoice Country of Origin column 'SE' (ISO alpha-2 normalization), and 'EU' confirmed via invoice exporter declaration stating 'EU preferential origin'. No conflicting non-EU origins in invoice. MATCH.

rule_evaluation_log.outcome: NOT_APPLICABLE – no LC reference found in certificate
Document A = Certificate of Origin, Document B = Invoice (2 pages). Single certificate, single invoice. Pairing straightforward.
PASSED – Consignor and Consignee both identified in invoice.
MATCH – 'Lantmännen Biorefineries AB' found in multiple locations in invoice

rule_evaluation_log.description: Country via indirect identifier (VAT prefix SE)

rule_evaluation_log.outcome: Customer address block identified as primary; Delivery Address as secondary. Both contain CHORI CO., LTD.
MATCH – 'Vital Wheat Gluten 1000 Kg' exact match
MATCH per point 2 – single value, no conflicting alternative
MATCH – EU Sweden verified against SE + EU preferential origin declaration

overall_assessment.human_explanation: Alla kontrollpunkter överensstämmer. Avsändaren Lantmännen Biorefineries AB bekräftas i fakturan, mottagaren CHORI CO., LTD finns i fakturans kundadress, varubeskrivningen 'Vital Wheat Gluten 1000 Kg' matchar exakt, kvantiteten 20 000 kg netto stämmer, och ursprungslandet EU/Sverige bekräftas via fakturans Country of Origin-fält (SE) och EU-ursprungsdeklaration. Dokumenten kan godkännas automatiskt.
overall_assessment.summary: All five checkpoints verified as MATCH. Consignor exactly identified as invoice issuer/exporter. Consignee exactly matched in Customer address and Delivery Address. Goods description 'Vital Wheat Gluten 1000 Kg' is an exact textual match. Quantity Net 20000 Kg confirmed (20,000 KG in invoice after thousands separator normalization). Origin 'EU Sweden' verified via Country of Origin column 'SE' and EU preferential origin exporter declaration.
non_blocking_observations: Certificate consignor address (Jungmansgatan 8, Lidköping) differs from invoice header address (Hanholmsvägen 69, Norrköping), but the exporter declaration in the invoice confirms the Lidköping address. Per rules, address differences do not affect consignor verification.
Invoice is a Pro Forma Invoice, not a commercial invoice. This is noted as an observation but does not affect verification.

sections.rule_applied: Priority identification (4.2.0.2) – Customer address is primary field. Name verification (4.2.2) – exact match. Country verification (4.2.3) – Japan matches.

sections.rule_applied: Controlled text matching (4.3.3) – identity-bearing main designation exact match. No article number in certificate, so Priority 1 not applicable.

sections.rule_applied: Occurrence check (4.4.1) with thousands separator normalization (4.4.3.3). Weight category single-value rule (4.4.2.2 point 2).

sections.invoice_value: SE (Country of Origin column); EU preferential origin (exporter declaration)
sections.rule_applied: EU [land] exception (4.5.4.2.1) – certificate 'EU Sweden' verified against invoice SE + EU preferential origin declaration. ISO normalization (4.5.4) – SE = Sweden.
sections.motivation: Both EU and Sweden components verified. Sweden confirmed via ISO code SE in Country of Origin column. EU confirmed via explicit exporter declaration 'EU preferential origin'. No non-EU origins in invoice.

human_readable_report.final_result: IDENTICAL – All checkpoints verified as MATCH. Document pair eligible for automatic approval.

output_quality_checks.notes: All 5 checkpoints MATCH with confidence ≥ 0.97. comparison_result = IDENTICAL is consistent. workflow_recommendation = AUTO_APPROVAL_ELIGIBLE is consistent with IDENTICAL.
